titleStmt.title: Letter from Alexander Gartlan to Henry Wynne, 8 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alexander Gartlan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Alexander Gartlan (1852 –1951), crown solicitor for County Louth, to to Sir Henry Arthur Wynne (1867–1945). Wynne was appointed to the vacant position of chief crown solicitor following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly (1851-1916).In this letter Gartlan requests a week of leave for a short holiday. Enclosed with the letter are Gartlan’s application and a letter of consent.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13688

correspAction.placeName: 12 Needham Place, Newry, County Down, Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
12, Needham Place, Newry 8th August 1916
re/ application for leave Dear Sir I hope to be able to get away next a week for a short holiday
and I would thank you to kindly bring the enclosed application before
the Attorney General for his permission. Of course if anything unexpectedly turned up during my absence
requiring my personal attention I would return when required. Yours faithfully, Alexander Gartlan The Chief Crown Solicitor The Castle Dublin. Attorney General
Do you approve?
H.S.W.
9.VIII.16
10th August 1916
Leave of Absence Dear Sir, The Attorney General consents to your having the leave
asked for. Yours truly, Henry Arthur Wynne Alexander Gartlan Esq., Crown Solicitor, Newry.
APPLICATION for LEAVE I ALEXANDER GARTLAND of 12 Needham Place Newry CROWN
SOLICITOR for the COUNTY OF LOUTH beg to apply for leave of
absence from 15th August 1916 to 5th September
1916. SCHEDULE TO BE FILLED UP
Proposed Substitute
My Managing Assistant
ANDREW CROMIE
NEWRY
âOR IN THE ALTERNATIVEâ
Maurice Molony
Dundalk Qualification
Solicitor
admitted (T) 1911
Solicitor
admitted (H) 1905 Crown Cases pending or which the Crown Solicitor has been
ordered to take charge of, which may possibly come on during
his absence:â Title of cause
None Nature of cause
nil Dated this 8th day of August 1916 Signed Alexander Gartlan
Solicitor 12 Needham Place Newry
